characteristics.name: Stability
characteristics.description: The unchanging ground beneath our feet, the foundation of all existence

characteristics.name: Fertility
characteristics.description: The womb of life, source of crops, food, and abundance

characteristics.name: Nurturing
characteristics.description: Mother Earth who feeds, shelters, and sustains all her children

characteristics.name: Endurance
characteristics.description: The patient strength of mountains, stones, and endless plains

characteristics.name: Grounding
characteristics.description: Connection to the physical, the material, the tangible reality

characteristics.name: Growth
characteristics.description: Seeds sprouting, roots deepening, life emerging from soil

characteristics.name: Cycles
characteristics.description: Seasons turning, death feeding new life, eternal renewal

characteristics.name: Body
characteristics.description: Physical form, flesh and bone, the material vessel

characteristics.name: Burial
characteristics.description: Return to earth, the final resting place, dust to dust

characteristics.name: Abundance
characteristics.description: Harvest, wealth, material prosperity from earth's bounty

keyMyths.title: Gaia's Birth and the Creation of the Titans
keyMyths.description: In Greek mythology, Gaia, the Earth, emerges from Chaos and gives birth to Uranus (the Sky), Pontus (the Sea), and the Titans. This act establishes the foundation of the cosmos and the initial divine order.

keyMyths.title: The Egyptian Creation Myth: Geb and Nut
keyMyths.description: Geb, the Earth god, and Nut, the Sky goddess, are locked in an embrace, preventing life from flourishing. Shu, the god of air, separates them, creating space for the world and allowing creation to proceed.

keyMyths.title: Pachamama and Andean Cosmology
keyMyths.description: Pachamama, the Earth Mother, is a central figure in Andean belief, representing the source of all life and sustenance. She is honored through offerings and rituals to ensure agricultural prosperity and the well-being of the community.
keyMyths.source: Guaman Poma de Ayala, *El Primer Nueva Corónica y Buen Gobierno*

keyMyths.title: The Māori Creation Story: Papa and Rangi
keyMyths.description: Papa, the Earth Mother, and Rangi, the Sky Father, are locked in a tight embrace, keeping their children, the gods, in darkness. Tāne Mahuta, god of the forest, separates them, bringing light and life to the world.
keyMyths.source: Grey, Sir George. *Polynesian Mythology and Ancient Traditional History of the New Zealand Race*

keyMyths.title: Thor's Battle with Jörmungandr
keyMyths.description: In Norse mythology, Thor's attempts to catch Jörmungandr, the Midgard Serpent encircling the Earth, highlight the constant struggle against chaos and the potential for the Earth's destruction during Ragnarök. This emphasizes the Earth's vulnerability.

sources.name: Hesiod, *Theogony*
sources.description: A foundational text of Greek mythology, providing an account of the origins of the gods and the cosmos.
sources.title: Hesiod, *Theogony*

sources.name: Egyptian Pyramid Texts
sources.description: A collection of ancient Egyptian funerary texts inscribed on pyramids, offering insights into their cosmology and beliefs about the afterlife.
sources.title: Egyptian Pyramid Texts

sources.name: Guaman Poma de Ayala, *El Primer Nueva Corónica y Buen Gobierno*
sources.description: A chronicle written by a native Andean nobleman, providing a valuable perspective on pre-Columbian and early colonial Andean culture and religion, including the worship of Pachamama.
sources.title: Guaman Poma de Ayala, *El Primer Nueva Corónica y Buen Gobierno*

sources.name: Grey, Sir George. *Polynesian Mythology and Ancient Traditional History of the New Zealand Race*
sources.description: A compilation of Māori myths and legends collected by Sir George Grey, a former Governor of New Zealand.
sources.title: Grey, Sir George. *Polynesian Mythology and Ancient Traditional History of the New Zealand Race*

sources.name: Snorri Sturluson, *Prose Edda*
sources.description: A 13th-century Icelandic textbook of Norse mythology, providing a comprehensive account of the gods, heroes, and cosmology of the Norse world.
sources.title: Snorri Sturluson, *Prose Edda*
